# @dynatrace-sdk/client-state

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-v1.9.2-blue)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dynatrace-sdk/client-state/v/1.9.2)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)

# Overview
Provides key-value storage for apps so that app developers can persist and get small chunks of
state in the context of their app.

States can either be stored in the context of an app (= app states) or in the context of an app and user (= user app states).
+ States stored per app can be read by every user of the app.
+ States stored per app and user can only be read and updated by the user who originally set that state.
Please visit the [Dynatrace Developer](https://dt-url.net/platform-services-state-service) to learn more about app states and user app states.



## Installation

```bash
npm install @dynatrace-sdk/client-state
```


## Getting help

- Visit [SDK for Typescript](https://developer.dynatrace.com/reference/sdks/) guide in the [Dynatrace Developer](https://developer.dynatrace.com/)
- Ask a question in the [Dynatrace Community](https://community.dynatrace.com/)


## License

This SDK is distributed under the [Apache License, Version 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0), see LICENSE for more information.


# API reference

Full API reference for the latest version of the SDK is also available at the [Dynatrace Developer](https://developer.dynatrace.com/reference/sdks/client-state/).

## stateClient

```js
import { stateClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-state';
```


<a name="deleteappstate"></a>
### deleteAppState

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.deleteAppState(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Deletes app state

**Required scope:** state:app-states:delete



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the key of the state |





#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|NotFound|Not found|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.deleteAppState({
  key: "some-key",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="deleteappstates"></a>
### deleteAppStates

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.deleteAppStates(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Delete all app states

**Required scope:** state:app-states:delete

Deletes all app states for an app to reset the app into a clean state.



</div>




#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.deleteAppStates();
```

</details>



</div>

<strong>stateClient.deleteUserAppState(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

| Error Type | Error Message |

|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|NotFound|Not found|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>

Code example
</summary>

```ts

const data = await stateClient.deleteUserAppState({
  key: "some-key",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="deleteuserappstates"></a>
### deleteUserAppStates

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.deleteUserAppStates(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Delete all user app states

**Required scope:** state:user-app-states:delete

Deletes all user app states for the calling user and app.



</div>




#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.deleteUserAppStates();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getappstate"></a>
### getAppState

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.getAppState(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#appstate">AppState</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets app state

**Required scope:** state:app-states:read



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the key of the state |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[AppState](#appstate)|200|The app state|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|NotFound|Not found|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.getAppState({
  key: "some-key",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getappstates"></a>
### getAppStates

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.getAppStates(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#appstates" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">AppStates</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

List app states

**Required scope:** state:app-states:read

Lists app states. By default, only provides property __key__ per state. Use *add-fields* parameter to include more fields and the *filter* parameter to narrow down the returned states.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.addFields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Provide a comma separated list of additional properties to be included in the response. |
|config.filter|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The filter parameter for filtering the set of returned resources If this parameter is omitted, no filtering is applied and all states will be returned.</p> <p>Filtering by string type parameters <code>key</code>, <code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedBy</code> when using one of the operators <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code> and <code>ends-with</code> is <strong>case insensitive</strong>.</p> <p>When using the operators <code>=</code>and <code>!=</code>, filtering is <strong>case sensitive</strong>.</p> <p>The following fields are legal filtering parameters - any other field names will result in a HTTP 400 response:</p> <ul> <li> <p><code>key</code>, supported operators: <code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code>, <code>ends-with</code>:</p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedTime</code>, supported operators:	<code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>&lt;</code>, <code>&lt;=</code>, <code>&gt;</code>, <code>&gt;=</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedBy</code>, supported operators: <code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code>, <code>ends-with</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>validUntilTime</code>, supported operators:	<code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>&lt;</code>, <code>&lt;=</code>, <code>&gt;</code>, <code>&gt;=</code></p> </li> </ul> <p>The following constraints apply:</p> <ul> <li> <p>Field names are <strong>case-sensitive</strong>.</p> </li> <li> <p>Conditions can be connected via operators <code>and</code> and <code>or</code>. A single condition can be negated by <code>not</code>.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strings <strong>must</strong> be enclosed in <strong>single quotes</strong>. e.g. <code>key contains 'my-string'</code></p> </li> <li> <p>Single quotes within a string must be escaped with a backslash: e.g. <code>key starts-with 'it\\'s a string'</code></p> </li> <li> <p>Maximum nesting depth (via brackets) is 2.</p> </li> <li> <p>Maximum length is 256 characters.</p> </li> </ul> <p>Examples:</p> <ul> <li> <p><code>key starts-with 'game-'</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedTime &gt;= '2022-07-01T00:10:05.000Z' and not (key contains 'new')</code></p> </li> </ul>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[AppStates](#appstates)|200|The list of app states|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.getAppStates();
```

</details>



</div>

<a name="getuserappstates"></a>
### getUserAppStates

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.getUserAppStates(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#userappstates" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">UserAppStates</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

List user app states

**Required scope:** state:user-app-states:read

Lists user app states. By default, only provides property __key__ per state. Use *add-fields* parameter to include more fields and the *filter* parameter to narrow down the returned states



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.addFields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Provide a comma separated list of additional properties to be included in the response. |
|config.filter|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The filter parameter for filtering the set of returned resources If this parameter is omitted, no filtering is applied and all states will be returned.</p> <p>Filtering by string type parameters <code>key</code>, <code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedBy</code> when using one of the operators <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code> and <code>ends-with</code> is <strong>case insensitive</strong>.</p> <p>When using the operators <code>=</code>and <code>!=</code>, filtering is <strong>case sensitive</strong>.</p> <p>The following fields are legal filtering parameters - any other field names will result in a HTTP 400 response:</p> <ul> <li> <p><code>key</code>, supported operators: <code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code>, <code>ends-with</code>:</p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedTime</code>, supported operators:	<code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>&lt;</code>, <code>&lt;=</code>, <code>&gt;</code>, <code>&gt;=</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedBy</code>, supported operators: <code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>contains</code>, <code>starts-with</code>, <code>ends-with</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>validUntilTime</code>, supported operators:	<code>=</code>, <code>!=</code>, <code>&lt;</code>, <code>&lt;=</code>, <code>&gt;</code>, <code>&gt;=</code></p> </li> </ul> <p>The following constraints apply:</p> <ul> <li> <p>Field names are <strong>case-sensitive</strong>.</p> </li> <li> <p>Conditions can be connected via operators <code>and</code> and <code>or</code>. A single condition can be negated by <code>not</code>.</p> </li> <li> <p>Strings <strong>must</strong> be enclosed in <strong>single quotes</strong>. e.g. <code>key contains 'my-string'</code></p> </li> <li> <p>Single quotes within a string must be escaped with a backslash: e.g. <code>key starts-with 'it\\'s a string'</code></p> </li> <li> <p>Maximum nesting depth (via brackets) is 2.</p> </li> <li> <p>Maximum length is 256 characters.</p> </li> </ul> <p>Examples:</p> <ul> <li> <p><code>key starts-with 'game-'</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>modificationInfo.lastModifiedTime &gt;= '2022-07-01T00:10:05.000Z' and not (key contains 'new')</code></p> </li> </ul>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[UserAppStates](#userappstates)|200|The list of user app states|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.getUserAppStates();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="setappstate"></a>
### setAppState

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.setAppState(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates app state

**Required scope:** state:app-states:write

Updates the *cross-user* app state for the given key. Be aware that other users will be able to read the value.
Use the user-scoped user-app-state to only store values for the authenticated user.

**[Certain limits](https://dt-url.net/platform-services-state-service/#limitations)** apply when updating app states.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#appstate">AppState</a>| |
|config.key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the key of the state |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|200|OK|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|AppStateLimitsExceeded|Exceeded size limit for combined size of app states of this app|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.setAppState({
  key: "some-key",
  body: { value: "some-state", validUntilTime: "now+2d" },
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="setuserappstate"></a>
### setUserAppState

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>stateClient.setUserAppState(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates user app state

**Required scope:** state:user-app-states:write

Updates the user specific app state for the given key and calling user.

**[Certain limits](https://dt-url.net/platform-services-state-service/#limitations)** apply when updating user app states.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#userappstate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">UserAppState</a>| |
|config.key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the key of the state |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|200|OK|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|UserAppStateLimitsExceeded|Exceeded limit for number of user app states to be stored for this user and app|
|Unauthorized|Unauthorized|
|Forbidden|Forbidden|
|InternalServerError|Internal server error|
|GeneralError|Unexpected error|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { stateClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-state";

const data = await stateClient.setUserAppState({
  key: "some-key",
  body: { value: "some-state", validUntilTime: "now+2d" },
});
```

</details>



</div>

## Types

### AppState

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|modificationInfo|<a href="#modificationinfo">ModificationInfo</a>| |
|validUntilTime|null &#124; [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the date until the state is persisted. Allowed are values from now+1m to now+90d! Returned validUntilTimes are always a string formatted in ISO 8601 |
|value<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |

### AppStates

**type**: Array&lt;<a href="#listappstate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ListAppState</a>&gt;

### Error

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|code<sup>*required</sup>|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)| 
|details|<a href="#errordetails">ErrorDetails</a>| 
|message<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|

### ErrorDetails

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|errorCode|"AppStateOverallSizeLimitExceeded" &#124; "AppStateSizeLimitExceeded" &#124; "UserAppStateSizeLimitExceeded" &#124; "UserAppStateCountLimitExceeded" &#124; "UserAppStateSizeLimitPerUserExceeded"|<p>Error code indicating the reason why the request failed</p> <ul> <li><code>AppStateOverallSizeLimitExceeded</code> - The overall size limit for the combined size of app states of this app was exceeded</li> <li><code>AppStateSizeLimitExceeded</code> - The app state value exceeded the size limit for a single app state</li> <li><code>UserAppStateSizeLimitPerUserExceeded</code> - The size of user app state values of a user for an app exceeds the limit</li> <li><code>UserAppStateSizeLimitExceeded</code> - <em><strong>deprecated</strong></em> The user app state content exceeded the size limit for a single user app state. No longer returned by the API.</li> <li><code>UserAppStateCountLimitExceeded</code> - <em><strong>deprecated</strong></em> The maximum number of user app states for this user and app was exceeded. No longer returned by the API.</li> </ul>  |

### ErrorResponse

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|error<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#error" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Error</a>|

### ListAppState

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |
|modificationInfo|<a href="#modificationinfo">ModificationInfo</a>| |
|validUntilTime|null &#124; [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the date until the state is persisted. Allowed are values from now+1m to now+90d! Returned validUntilTimes are always a string formatted in ISO 8601 |
|value|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |

### ListUserAppState

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|key<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |
|modificationInfo|<a href="#modificationinfo" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ModificationInfo</a>| |
|validUntilTime|null &#124; [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the date until the state is persisted. Allowed are values from now+1m to now+90d! Returned validUntilTimes are always a string formatted in ISO 8601 |
|value|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |

### ModificationInfo

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|lastModifiedBy<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| 
|lastModifiedTime<sup>*required</sup>|Date|

### PersistenceState

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|modificationInfo|<a href="#modificationinfo">ModificationInfo</a>| |
|validUntilTime|null &#124; [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the date until the state is persisted. Allowed are values from now+1m to now+90d! Returned validUntilTimes are always a string formatted in ISO 8601 |
|value|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |

### UserAppState

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|modificationInfo|<a href="#modificationinfo" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ModificationInfo</a>| |
|validUntilTime|null &#124; [string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Specify the date until the state is persisted. Allowed are values from now+1m to now+90d! Returned validUntilTimes are always a string formatted in ISO 8601 |
|value<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| |

### UserAppStates

**type**: Array&lt;<a href="#listuserappstate">ListUserAppState</a>&gt;

## Enums

<a name="errordetailserrorcode"></a>
### ~~ErrorDetailsErrorCode~~

> ⚠️ **Deprecated**
> Use literal values.

<div class="padding-left--md">

Error code indicating the reason why the request failed
  * `AppStateOverallSizeLimitExceeded` - The overall size limit for the combined size of app states of this app was exceeded
  * `AppStateSizeLimitExceeded` - The app state value exceeded the size limit for a single app state
  * `UserAppStateSizeLimitPerUserExceeded` - The size of user app state values of a user for an app exceeds the limit
  * `UserAppStateSizeLimitExceeded` - ___deprecated___ The user app state content exceeded the size limit for a single user app state. No longer returned by the API.
  * `UserAppStateCountLimitExceeded` - ___deprecated___ The maximum number of user app states for this user and app was exceeded. No longer returned by the API.

#### Enum keys

<div class="padding-left--md">

`AppStateOverallSizeLimitExceeded` | `AppStateSizeLimitExceeded` | `UserAppStateCountLimitExceeded` | `UserAppStateSizeLimitExceeded` | `UserAppStateSizeLimitPerUserExceeded`

</div>


</div>
